reasoning:
The stock recently experienced a significant drop following an April earnings miss, falling to a 52-week low of ~342. Short-term momentum is distinctly negative, justifying probabilities slightly below 0.50 for the nearest time horizons (1 week, 1 month). The linear trend analysis yielded a short-term probability of ~0.48, which aligns closely with the expectation of continued downside pressure or very weak bounce. 

However, over longer time horizons (3 to 6 months), the positive drift of the broader equity market and mean reversion back to historical averages usually begin to dominate, reducing the impact of short-term negative momentum. The probability thus drifts closer to 0.50 for the 3-month horizon, and turns slightly positive (>0.50) for the 6-month horizon due to the standard positive drift of broad equities combined with increased uncertainty. This structure accurately reflects increasing forecast horizon uncertainty (converging near 0.50) while respecting the current momentum profile.
